title: Threads, Atomics, Data Races, and Safe Reclamation · Undefined-Behavior Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted threaded lifetime and reclamation proof, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in threaded lifetime and reclamation proof and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify conflicting non-atomic accesses without happens-before ordering as undefined behavior.
Select atomic operations and memory order from a stated synchronization proof instead of folklore.
Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Race Undefined
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug native-systems team handles memory case 4235. Produce bounded C evidence to model publication and reclamation state without claiming thread proof; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify conflicting non-atomic accesses without happens-before ordering as undefined behavior.
